module.title: ACC SRVY 1301 — Introduction to Surveying
module.description: Austin Community College SRVY 1301 (3 SCH). An overview of the surveying profession covering the history of surveying and its impact on the world, review of mathematics used in surveying, introduction to basic surveying equipment with emphasis on horizontal and vertical measurements, instruction on surveying procedures and limitation of errors, and calculations to determine precision and error of closure. Topics include: units and significant figures, field notes, theory of errors, leveling theory and field procedures, distance measurement, angles/azimuths/bearings, total stations, traversing, traverse computations, coordinate geometry, area computation, and mapping surveys. Textbook: Elementary Surveying by Ghilani or Surveying Fundamentals by Nathanson. No prerequisites.

lessons.title: Week 1: Introduction to Surveying
Week 2: Units, Significant Figures, and Field Notes
Week 3: Theory of Errors in Observation
Week 4: Leveling — Theory, Methods, and Equipment
Week 5: Leveling — Field Procedures and Computations
Week 6: Distance Measurement
Week 7: Angles, Azimuths, and Bearings
Week 8: Midterm Exam
Week 9: Total Station Instruments and Angle Observations
Week 10: Traversing
Week 11: Traverse Computations
Week 12: Coordinate Geometry in Surveying
Week 13: Area Computation
Week 14: Mapping Surveys and Map Drafting
Week 15: Boundary Surveys and Public Lands
Week 16: Final Exam

module.title: ACC SRVY 1335 — Land Surveying Applications (Lab)
module.description: Austin Community College SRVY 1335 (3 SCH). An intermediate lab course covering electronic distance measuring equipment, total stations, theodolites, and data collection methods. Includes monument and corner establishment. Students gain practical field experience with equipment, techniques, and hardware necessary to measure horizontal and vertical angles and distances used in surveying. This is the lab companion to SRVY 1341 and must be taken concurrently. Topics include: EDM equipment operation, total station setup and data recording, theodolite angular measurement, horizontal and vertical angle field procedures, traverse field work, differential leveling, profile and cross-section leveling, monument setting, and a comprehensive field survey project. Prerequisite: SRVY 1301. Corequisite: SRVY 1341.

lessons.title: Week 1: Lab Safety, Equipment Overview, and Field Book Setup
Week 2: Electronic Distance Measuring (EDM) Equipment
Week 3: Total Station Setup and Operation
Week 4: Theodolite Operation and Angular Measurement
Week 5: Horizontal Angle Measurement Lab
Week 6: Vertical Angle Measurement and Zenith Angles
Week 7: Distance Measurement Techniques — Taping and EDM Comparison
Week 8: Midterm Practical Exam
Week 9: Open Traverse Field Procedures
Week 10: Closed Traverse Field Procedures
Week 11: Data Collection Methods and Electronic Recording
Week 12: Differential Leveling Field Lab
Week 13: Monument and Corner Establishment
Week 14: Profile and Cross-Section Leveling
Week 15: Comprehensive Field Survey Project
Week 16: Final Practical Exam

module.title: ACC SRVY 1341 — Land Surveying
module.description: Austin Community College SRVY 1341 (3 SCH). A study of the measurement and determination of boundaries, areas, shapes, and location through traversing techniques. Instruction in a variety of adjustment methods using programmed and non-programmed hand-held calculators and computers. Methods of traversing and adjustment of errors according to prevailing and applicable professional standards and the standards of the Texas Board of Professional Engineers and Land Surveyors. Topics include: traverse types and planning, latitudes and departures, error of closure and precision ratios, compass rule and transit rule adjustments, coordinate geometry and inverse computations, area computation methods (coordinate and DMD), boundary determination and retracement principles, subdivision concepts, and professional standards compliance. Prerequisite: SRVY 1301. Corequisite: SRVY 1335.

lessons.title: Welcome to SRVY 1341 — Land Surveying
Week 1: Distance Measurement — Chaining & Tape Corrections
Week 2: Compass Surveying, DD/DMS Conversions & Chaining Review
Week 3: Magnetic Declination Review, Smartphone Compass Apps, Field Notekeeping & Trade Magazines
Week 4: Instrument Care, Tripod Best Practices & Maintenance Schedules
Week 5: Instrument Leveling & Setup Over a Point
Week 6: Types of Angles & Angle Measurement
Week 7: Transit Rule and Other Adjustment Methods
Week 8: Midterm Exam
Week 9: Coordinate Geometry — Inversing and Intersections
Week 10: Area Computation — Coordinate and DMD Methods
Week 11: Boundary Determination I — Evidence and Monuments
Week 12: Boundary Determination II — Retracement Principles
Week 13: Subdivision Concepts and Curve Data
Week 14: Computer Applications in Traverse Adjustment
Week 15: Professional Standards and Comprehensive Review
Week 16: Final Exam

module.title: ACC SRVY 2343 — Surveying Legal Principles I
module.description: Austin Community College SRVY 2343 (3 SCH). A study of the laws governing boundaries and land ownership as they apply to the land surveyor. Topics include real property concepts, estates and ownership types, deed construction and interpretation, legal descriptions (metes and bounds, lot-and-block, PLSS), the Texas land system and its unique history of Spanish and Mexican land grants, boundary evidence and the priority of calls, riparian and littoral boundaries, easements and right-of-way, adverse possession and acquiescence, the role of the surveyor as expert witness, and professional ethics in boundary determination. This course provides the legal foundation necessary for the Surveyor-in-Training (SIT) and RPLS examinations. Prerequisite: SRVY 1341. Textbook: Evidence and Procedures for Boundary Location by Brown, Robillard, and Wilson.

lessons.title: Week 1: Introduction to Property Law and the Surveyor's Role
Week 2: Estates and Ownership in Real Property
Week 3: Deeds — Construction, Types, and Interpretation
Week 4: Legal Descriptions — Metes and Bounds
Week 5: Legal Descriptions — PLSS and Lot-and-Block
Week 6: The Texas Land System — Spanish and Mexican Grants
Week 7: Boundary Evidence and the Priority of Calls
Week 8: Midterm Exam
Week 9: Original Surveys vs Retracement Surveys
Week 10: Riparian and Littoral Boundaries
Week 11: Adverse Possession and Acquiescence
Week 12: Easements and Rights-of-Way
Week 13: The Surveyor as Expert Witness
Week 14: Professional Ethics and Texas Survey Standards
Week 15: Case Law Review and Comprehensive Exam Preparation
Week 16: Final Exam
